info.title: data-engine API
info.description:
Runtime-backed OpenAPI surface for labeling and eval operations. Only routes
dispatched by the current server are published for generated clients.
One operationId per endpoint, deterministic names, explicit replay contracts, and operation envelopes.
JSON request bodies are route-capped before read; body-bearing methods require exactly one ASCII-decimal
Content-Length, while ambiguous framing and every Transfer-Encoding are rejected with connection close.
New artifact and pipeline canonical envelopes are capped at 16 MiB.
Labeling jobs and pipelines have aggregate monotonic deadlines, persistent resource rows have project-scoped
live-state caps, and expert resolutions atomically create accepted human labels under an exact replay key.

get.summary: Health check

post.summary: MCP JSON-RPC 2.0 endpoint projecting the REST operations as tools
post.description: Stateless JSON-RPC 2.0 endpoint (initialize, ping, tools/list, tools/call). Tools are projected from the REST operations; a tools/call dispatches through the same bearer auth (static or central introspection, audience data-engine) and route code path as REST. Notifications (requests without an id) are acknowledged with 202 and no body; non-POST methods return 405 with Allow: POST. Tool application failures are JSON-RPC results with isError true and the canonical REST error envelope in structuredContent. structuredContent is authoritative; text content is a bounded summary rather than a duplicate payload. JSON-RPC error is reserved for parse, protocol, parameter, and dispatch failures. The server negotiates MCP protocol version 2025-06-18. Every request after initialize must send MCP-Protocol-Version: 2025-06-18. Browser requests with Origin are accepted only when the exact origin appears in DATA_ENGINE_MCP_ALLOWED_ORIGINS; non-browser clients may omit Origin. The global wire ceiling is 17 MiB. Control methods are capped at 4 KiB, initialize and read-tool calls at 64 KiB, and write tools at the REST route body limit plus a 16 KiB envelope allowance.

parameters.name: MCP-Protocol-Version
parameters.description: Required on every non-initialize request after version negotiation; initialize omits it and declares protocolVersion in params.

requestBody.description: JSON request body; rejected before read when Content-Length exceeds 17 MiB or its smaller method/tool limit.

405.description: Method not allowed (only POST is supported)

get.summary: List MVP data products and pipeline templates
Get one MVP use-case template

post.summary: Ingest one or many artifacts

requestBody.description: JSON request body; rejected before read when Content-Length exceeds 17 MiB.

post.summary: Fetch, parse, and ingest a public HTTP/HTTPS page

requestBody.description: JSON request body; rejected before read when Content-Length exceeds 17 MiB.

post.summary: Create a data campaign with rubric, budget, target accuracy, and skill tags

requestBody.description: JSON request body; rejected before read when Content-Length exceeds 64 KiB.

get.summary: List data campaigns

post.summary: Pause, resume, or permanently close campaign job admission

requestBody.description: JSON request body; rejected before read when Content-Length exceeds 64 KiB.

200.description: Immutable receipt for the committed lifecycle transition

get.summary: Get the authenticated reviewer's campaign qualification
get.description: Returns only the caller's project-scoped pseudonymous explicit-gold history and campaign eligibility. Blind probe results are deliberately excluded so before/after queries cannot reveal a hidden probe outcome.

200.description: Reviewer qualification under the immutable campaign policy

post.summary: Run a complete MVP use-case pipeline

requestBody.description: JSON request body; rejected before read when Content-Length exceeds 17 MiB.

200.description: Pipeline result with campaign, artifacts, labels, bundle, and metrics

get.summary: List human residual review tasks

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Restrict the queue to actionable or completed tasks.

parameters.name: campaignName
parameters.description: Restrict tasks to one exact campaign resource name.

post.summary: Clone a real review task into an isolated HMAC-scored qualification task
post.description: Requires review:gold:manage. The expected label is write-only and is retained only as a keyed HMAC under the private reviewer key. The clone preserves the source artifact, campaign, rubric, and task family but receives an isolated label cohort that no job or product references.

requestBody.description: Bounded gold-management request; expected_label is never returned.

post.summary: Resolve, abstain, flag, or adjudicate one human residual

requestBody.description: The idempotency key binds one exact normalized decision to one expert task.

200.description: Resolved task and its recorded resolve, abstain, flag, or adjudication observation

post.summary: Atomically claim one open expert task with an exclusive renewable lease
post.description: Returns retryable 503 UNAVAILABLE while assignment lifecycle writes are disabled in enforce-only rollback mode; existing leases remain authoritative.

post.summary: Renew the authenticated reviewer's active task lease
post.description: Returns retryable 503 UNAVAILABLE while assignment lifecycle writes are disabled in enforce-only rollback mode; existing leases remain authoritative.

post.summary: Release the authenticated reviewer's active task lease for reassignment
post.description: Returns retryable 503 UNAVAILABLE while assignment lifecycle writes are disabled in enforce-only rollback mode; existing leases remain authoritative.

post.summary: Autosave a version-checked draft under the active reviewer lease
post.description: Returns retryable 503 UNAVAILABLE while assignment lifecycle writes are disabled in enforce-only rollback mode; existing leases remain authoritative.

200.description: Assignment carrying the persisted draft and incremented version

get.summary: Get project-scoped human-review operations and quality measures
get.description: Returns queue and SLA observations, bounded-window throughput, task-family-specific exact agreement with Wilson 95% intervals, pseudonymous reviewer calibration against consensus or adjudicated finals, rubric-version drift, and declared campaign budget. These observational measures never mutate or promote a review decision, and actual reviewer cost is explicitly unavailable until authoritative cost receipts exist. Quality aggregation fails closed above 50,000 accepted resolved task rows and all response dimensions are explicitly bounded.

parameters.name: windowSeconds
parameters.description: Bounded lookback window for throughput counters.

parameters.name: slaTargetSeconds
parameters.description: Observational queue-age threshold. This does not create or replace a durable campaign SLA policy.

get.summary: Get data-engine usage and quality metrics for a project
Get the label quality report for a project
get.description: Per-verifier stats (label counts, mean confidence, accepted rate), cross-verifier disagreements for artifacts whose labels differ (capped at 50), the needs_expert backlog, and the overall auto_resolution_rate (consistent with the project metrics).

get.summary: Get public-site and ecosystem readiness for one project
List artifacts
Get an artifact

200.description: Verified artifact summary by default, or full artifact for view=FULL

get.summary: List labels for an artifact

post.summary: Profile dataset quality before export
post.description: Deterministic quality profile over the whole project or an explicit artifact_ids subset: counts by artifact type and source, duplicate raw_hash groups, label coverage, and confidence/decision/label-value distributions computed over the latest label per artifact.

post.summary: Create a labeling job

requestBody.description: JSON request body; rejected before read when Content-Length exceeds 512 KiB. New campaign jobs require ACTIVE. Completed exact idempotency retries are returned before the lifecycle gate when a campaign is paused or closed.

get.summary: Get a job
List deterministic results for a job
Get product

post.summary: Validate an emitted product bundle
post.description: Referential-integrity and hygiene check for a bundle. Missing or out-of-project artifacts/labels and a missing/unparseable manifest are errors; duplicate raw_hash groups within the bundle and included needs_expert labels are warnings.

post.summary: Check raw_hash leakage between two product bundles
post.description: Train/eval split hygiene: computes the raw_hash overlap between exactly two bundles, including overlap ratios relative to each bundle and the overlapping hashes (capped at 50).

get.summary: Get an integrity-checked eval product manifest

post.summary: Admit an exact product generation for model training
post.description: Requires the dedicated training:publish scope. READY eval publication alone is insufficient: admission revalidates integrity and live review state, requires explicit model_training consent on every resolved human review, and binds a different READY heldout product with zero raw-hash overlap. Exact retries revalidate the captured generations.

requestBody.description: JSON request body; rejected before read when Content-Length exceeds 16 KiB.

get.summary: Revalidate and get a training release
get.description: Requires the dedicated training:publish scope. Revalidates both exact product generations and durably returns STALE if review, consent, integrity, leakage, or generation state no longer authorizes training.

post.summary: Derive a post-training bundle (SFT or preference) from a READY product
post.description: Read-only, deterministic derivation from the integrity-checked product manifest. The train/val split is content-addressed (hash of each artifact's raw bytes), so identical content always lands in the same split across products and replays. sft emits one record per accepted item with the authoritative label as the completion; preference emits chosen/rejected pairs from expert overrides and disagreeing ensemble minority votes. Requires quality_status READY; bounded keyset pages.

200.description: One bounded page of derived post-training records

post.summary: Emit eval bundle

requestBody.description: JSON request body; rejected before read when Content-Length exceeds 512 KiB.

post.summary: Extract from a source connector (s3, snowflake, salesforce) into artifacts
post.description: Pulls objects/records from a configured source connector and (by default) ingests them as content-addressed artifacts. Text S3 objects ingest raw; binary S3 objects are base64-encoded into raw_body with an encoding marker; structured rows (snowflake, salesforce) ingest as JSON row batches of 100 with row-count metadata. Fails closed with 412 FAILED_PRECONDITION when the connector's env config is absent.

get.summary: List source connectors with configured/unconfigured state
get.description: Reports each source connector (s3, snowflake, salesforce) as configured or unconfigured booleans plus non-secret config (region, endpoint host, api version). Secret values are never echoed.

post.summary: Create or version-bump a stored custom tool
post.description: The tool forge write surface. Identical re-creates are idempotent; a changed definition for an existing name creates a new monotonic version with every previous version retained. Definitions are capped at 64 KiB canonical JSON and 256 tools per project. Stored tools are advertised dynamically on the MCP catalog as custom.<project>.<name>.

get.summary: List stored custom tools with usage stats
get.description: Lists every stored tool for the project, including invocation_count/last_invoked_at/error_count so an operator agent can prune stale tools.

get.summary: Get a stored custom tool (definition + stats)

delete.description: Deletes the tool row and every retained version; the deletion is recorded in the custom tool audit log with the final usage stats.

post.summary: Invoke a stored custom tool
post.description: deterministic_wasm tools execute only in the cradle sandbox (empty policy, no egress) and record program_sha256 plus the sandbox inputs_digest; when DATA_ENGINE_CRADLE_URL is unset the invoke fails closed with 412 FAILED_PRECONDITION. llm_prompt tools render the stored template with the caller arguments (no secret interpolation) and run the NVIDIA client (offline dry-run yields determinism "heuristic").

post.summary: Atomically commit an immutable discovery release graph
post.description: Admits an exact, same-project graph of already-ingested artifacts. Artifact type, raw SHA-256, release policy, lineage, eligibility, and integer budget invariants are validated in one transaction. Replaying the same requestId and semantic content returns the original release; reuse with different content fails with ALREADY_EXISTS. This operation records evidence and does not authorize or perform a physical experiment.

get.summary: Get one immutable discovery release

post.summary: Create an immutable shared evidence record
post.description: Creates a domain-neutral record by canonical content hash. Source and receipt artifacts must already exist in the same project. VERIFIED claims require at least one retained VERIFIER_RECEIPT artifact. Creation requires the eval scope rather than ordinary dataset write authority.

get.summary: List immutable shared evidence records
Get one immutable shared evidence record

post.summary: Create an immutable shared episode
post.description: Creates an episode by canonical content hash. Its context evidence and any verifier receipt artifacts must already exist in the same project; PASS results require a retained VERIFIER_RECEIPT artifact. Creation requires the eval scope rather than ordinary dataset write authority.

get.summary: List immutable shared episodes
Get one immutable shared episode

post.summary: Retrieve candidates for an exact canonical typed obligation
post.description: Read-only structured query over one project's immutable catalog snapshot. The content hash is recomputed using tempera.math-ast-c14n/v1 before any catalog metadata is returned. A result is a retrieval candidate, not a proof or an invocation authorization.

post.summary: Create an immutable executable research catalog entry
post.description: Creates by canonical content hash. An exact retry returns the same resource; entries cannot be mutated or deleted through this API.

get.summary: List immutable executable research catalog entries
Get one immutable executable research catalog entry

bearerAuth.description: Central Tempera bearer token. Resource servers validate hosted access tokens or API keys through the control-plane introspection endpoint; local smoke tests may use DATA_ENGINE_API_TOKEN.

ParentParam.name: parent
ParentParam.description: Resource parent in the form projects/{project}

ToolName.name: toolName
ToolName.description: Stored custom tool name (snake_case, unique per project)

IfMatchHeader.name: If-Match
IfMatchHeader.description: Required for PATCH and archive operations with mutable state changes

RequestIdParam.name: x-request-id
RequestIdParam.description: Reserved caller trace hint; success responses do not currently echo it

IdempotencyKeyHeader.name: Idempotency-Key
IdempotencyKeyHeader.description: Campaign create/transition replay key. It must match a body idempotencyKey when both are sent; transition requests still require the body field.

AipPageTokenParam.name: pageToken
AipPageTokenParam.description: Opaque AIP-158 continuation token from nextPageToken.

Forbidden.description: Authenticated but not authorized (audience or scope)

Conflict.description: Immutable identity conflict; ALREADY_EXISTS is non-retryable

ProductNotReady.description: Product publication is incomplete; FAILED_PRECONDITION is retryable

ProductPrecondition.description: Product publication is retryably incomplete or human-data reuse policy failed non-retryably

TrainingReleasePrecondition.description: Training release idempotency conflict, stale generation, or policy failure; non-retryable

DataLoss.description: Committed artifact raw bytes failed integrity verification; non-retryable

PayloadTooLarge.description: Declared JSON request body exceeds its route or tool pre-read limit (17 MiB global maximum)

FailedPrecondition.description: Precondition failed (for example, a source connector's env config is absent)

Unavailable.description: Retryable service or write-path unavailability; inspect the error message before retrying

TooManyRequests.description: Capacity or live-state quota error; inspect retryable before retrying

code.description: HTTP status code for the failed request.

reason.description: Stable Data Engine reason for the error.

metadata.description: Includes requestId and retryable correlation metadata.
Bounded, non-secret structured diagnostics for this error.

verifierReceiptArtifactRefs.description: Required and non-empty for VERIFIED evidence; every item must also appear in artifact_refs.

path.description: Canonical slash-separated Tempera capability graph path.

contentHash.description: Optional precondition; when supplied it must equal the server's canonical identity hash.

ResearchExpression.description: Runtime validation enforces constructor-specific fields, arity, depth 64, and 4096 total nodes per obligation.

ResourceRef.description: project-scoped resource name, for example projects/acme/artifacts/a1

metadata.description: Producer metadata is retained and participates in immutable content identity.
Producer metadata is retained and participates in immutable content identity.

ArtifactIngestRequest.description: Generic artifact input. remi.memory_feedback.v1 markers are excluded from this branch and must use RemiMemoryFeedbackIngestRequest's exact canonical wire shape.

artifactType.description: Artifact type. An explicit blank is invalid.

externalId.description: Source-unique identity. Defaults to a random uuid. Reusing the same project, source, and externalId with identical content returns the existing artifact; different content returns ALREADY_EXISTS (409). An explicit blank is invalid.

rawBody.description: Raw payload. The full canonical artifact envelope must not exceed 16 MiB.

metadata.description: Producer metadata is retained and participates in immutable content identity.

RemiMemoryFeedbackIngestRequest.description: Exact Remi v1 handoff. The receiver recomputes externalId from these fields and the target data-engine project, enforces disclosure consistency, and caps canonical content at 256 KiB.

tenantId.description: One to 256 UTF-8 bytes; maxLength is the closest JSON Schema character bound.

projectId.description: One to 256 UTF-8 bytes; maxLength is the closest JSON Schema character bound.

environmentId.description: Null or one to 256 UTF-8 bytes; maxLength is the closest JSON Schema character bound.

nodeId.description: One to 256 UTF-8 bytes; maxLength is the closest JSON Schema character bound.

text.description: Optional; present exactly when policy.memory_text_included is true. Runtime enforces 65,536 UTF-8 bytes; maxLength is the closest JSON Schema character bound.

noteSha256.description: Present together with note exactly when feedback_note_included is true.

note.description: At most 4,096 UTF-8 bytes whose digest must equal note_sha256; maxLength is the closest JSON Schema character bound.

metadata.description: Producer metadata is retained and participates in immutable content identity.

url.description: Default external identity. URLs longer than the external-id limit use a bounded SHA-256 identity; the full URL remains in the verified raw body and metadata.

producerVersion.description: Optional immutable producer version such as an ETag, commit, or snapshot id. Omitted requests retain the URL-only identity for backward compatibility. Supply a new version when content at the URL changes; reusing a version with different content returns ALREADY_EXISTS (409). The receiver derives a bounded SHA-256 external identity rather than embedding the URL or version.

metadata.description: Producer metadata is retained and participates in immutable content identity.

budgetCents.description: Planning metadata only in this release; the receiver does not yet meter or stop work against this value.

requiredReviews.description: Independently authenticated review slots created for each residual label.

routingPolicy.description: Immutable queue routing policy snapshotted onto each created task.

annotationSchema.description: Additive structured-annotation metadata. Older producer revisions may omit this together with annotation_schema_version during a consumer-first rollout.

budgetCents.description: Planning metadata only in this release; the receiver does not yet meter or stop work against this value.

idempotencyKey.description: MCP/body form of the durable campaign replay key; must match the header when both are sent.

mode.description: Shadow measures explicit qualification without blocking claims; required enforces the thresholds; disabled uses zero thresholds.

priority.description: Higher values are more urgent within a project queue.

taskSlaSeconds.description: Task deadline offset snapshotted at task creation.

batchKey.description: Opaque project-scoped batch grouping; never a reviewer identity.

maxOpenTasks.description: Maximum OPEN review tasks for this campaign; job admission reserves worst-case reviewer fanout before verifier work.

targetStatus.description: PAUSED blocks new jobs, ACTIVE resumes admission, and CLOSED is terminal.

idempotencyKey.description: Exact retries return the original immutable transition receipt.

requiresInputArtifacts.description: When true, pipeline runs fail closed unless the caller supplies authorized source artifacts; the catalog does not provide examples as production inputs.

id.description: Non-null request id. Omit for a notification (server replies 202, no body).

method.description: Requests implement initialize, ping, tools/list, and tools/call; initialize requires protocolVersion, capabilities, and clientInfo with bounded name/version. Bounded notifications such as notifications/initialized are acknowledged without dispatch.

result.description: Tool results include authoritative structuredContent and isError. Text content is a bounded summary, not a duplicate serialized payload. Application failures use isError true and carry the canonical REST error envelope in structuredContent.

code.description: JSON-RPC parse, protocol, parameter, or dispatch error code.

data.description: Optional protocol or dispatch error context.

verifier.description: Verifier backend for the pipeline. Defaults to nvidia; cradle runs the use-case's wasm verifier in the cradle sandbox; agent dispatches the configured coding agent (DATA_ENGINE_AGENT_CMD, fail-closed 412 when unset); ensemble majority-votes across the tempera-llm gateway models (TEMPERA_LLM_GATEWAY_URL / DATA_ENGINE_ENSEMBLE_MODELS, fail-closed 412 when unset).

budgetCents.description: Planning metadata only in this release; the receiver does not yet meter or stop work against this value.

artifacts.description: Producer artifacts are admitted before campaign creation. Built-in, producer, and URL artifacts have a combined limit of 64 and 16 MiB of canonical artifact content. Static validation and all URL fetches finish before admission. Reusing a source identity with changed content returns ALREADY_EXISTS (409) without creating a campaign.

verifier.description: The verifier backend that produced the labels for this run.

rubric.description: Immutable rubric snapshot bound when the task is created.

rubricVersion.description: Content digest of the immutable rubric snapshot.

annotationSchema.description: Additive structured-annotation metadata. Older producer revisions may omit this together with annotation_schema_version during a consumer-first rollout.

annotationSchemaVersion.description: Content digest of the immutable structured annotation schema.

priority.description: Immutable priority snapshot inherited from the campaign routing policy.

deadlineTime.description: Immutable SLA deadline snapshot; null only for legacy tasks created before routing policy support.

batchKey.description: Immutable opaque batch snapshot inherited from the campaign routing policy.

reviewRole.description: Adjudication tasks are created when independent accepted reviews disagree.

qualificationTask.description: True only for disclosed onboarding tasks. Blind gold probes always return false and are otherwise indistinguishable from ordinary tasks.

outcome.description: Adjudicate is accepted only when review_role is adjudication; abstain and flag create one independent replacement with the same review role.

annotation.description: Required for span, region, ranking, pairwise, and taxonomy decisions. Legacy and explicit classification tasks may derive the one selected label when omitted. The receiver validates the value against the immutable task annotation_schema and includes it in replay identity.

annotatorId.description: Optional echo of the authenticated reviewer. REST binds identity from a central token subject or trusted act-as subject and rejects a mismatch; direct static-token review writes are forbidden. The value becomes a project-scoped keyed pseudonym before persistence, and one reviewer cannot satisfy multiple independent slots.

idempotencyKey.description: Hashed before persistence and bound to the normalized resolution payload.

leaseToken.description: Opaque client-generated lease capability. Required once this task has entered the assignment lifecycle; only its SHA-256 digest is persisted.

reuseTargets.description: Must contain one to four targets when authorized is true and must be empty when authorized is false.

contentSha256.description: Required for artifact provenance and verified against the retained raw object.

verification.description: Receiver-added trust classification; callers cannot submit this field.

authorized.description: Caller assertion of reuse consent. False preserves the operational review but prevents product reuse. The receiver preserves and enforces the declaration but does not authenticate an external controller policy or consent receipt.

verification.description: Receiver-added trust classification; callers cannot submit this field.

expectedLabel.description: Exact expected decision. The receiver retains only a keyed HMAC and never returns the clear value or its digest.

feedbackPolicy.description: Blind mode requires none; result_only never returns the expected label.

evaluatedGoldTasks.description: Explicit qualification tasks only; blind probes are excluded.

ReviewQualificationResult.description: Present only for explicit tasks whose policy permits result-only feedback. Never present for blind probes and never contains the expected label.

idempotencyKey.description: Hashed before persistence and binds an exact claim or renewal request.

leaseToken.description: Opaque client-generated capability; only its SHA-256 digest is persisted.

draft.description: Bounded structured reviewer form state, persisted only in Data Engine.

expectedVersion.description: Optimistic concurrency version returned by the active assignment.

assigneeRef.description: Project-scoped keyed reviewer pseudonym; clear identity is never returned.

leaseExpiresAt.description: Unix epoch seconds. It is meaningful only while state is ACTIVE.

reviewerCalibration.description: Project-keyed pseudonymous rows suppressed until a reviewer has at least five resolved and five consensus-eligible reviews in the task family.

staleActiveLeases.description: ACTIVE rows whose lease timestamp is already past as_of.

unassignedOpenTasks.description: Open tasks without a non-expired ACTIVE lease.

slaAtRiskTasks.description: Open tasks aged from 80% up to the supplied SLA threshold.

slaBreachedTasks.description: Open tasks at or beyond the supplied SLA threshold.

reviewerRef.description: Project-scoped reviewer pseudonym; clear identity is never returned.

blindTasks.description: Count only; blind identities and outcomes are never projected.

explicitEvaluatedTasks.description: Completed explicit result_only tasks eligible for aggregate feedback.

explicitCorrectTasks.description: Suppressed with accuracy and interval until at least five eligible result_only tasks exist; feedback_policy none never contributes.

explicitGoldAccuracy.description: Result-only aggregate suppressed below five eligible evaluations.

drifted.description: True when the task family has more than one immutable rubric version.

actualCostAvailable.description: False until authoritative reviewer-cost receipts exist.

determinism.description: How the label was decided: deterministic (cradle-wasm sandbox, schema/exact-match), heuristic (offline dry-run rules), or model (LLM verifier judgment).

taskFamily.description: agent-memory-feedback is reserved for selections containing only remi.memory_feedback.v1 artifacts.

idempotencyKey.description: Exact retries reuse one job. Required when any selected artifact has the remi.memory_feedback.v1 contract.

verifier.description: Verifier backend; defaults to nvidia. agent dispatches the configured coding agent (DATA_ENGINE_AGENT_CMD, fail-closed 412 when unset); ensemble majority-votes across the tempera-llm gateway models (TEMPERA_LLM_GATEWAY_URL / DATA_ENGINE_ENSEMBLE_MODELS, fail-closed 412 when unset).

manifestPath.description: Internal compatibility field; remote callers should use manifest_url.

manifestUrl.description: Project-scoped API path for the integrity-checked manifest.

manifestSha256.description: SHA-256 for new manifests; empty only on migrated legacy rows.

schema.description: Present on job-scoped manifests; absent on migrated legacy manifests.

metrics.description: Product-scoped metrics on v1 manifests; project metrics on migrated legacy manifests.

candidateReviewedAt.description: Immutable review time for a human authoritative label; null for automated labels.

reviewLabelNames.description: All unanimous human review labels bound into the selected candidate.

ProductSourcePolicy.description:
Typed source policy retained from the selected artifact. Generated
clients must accept every policy shape the product manifest can emit.

producerOriginVerification.description: Shape and retained bytes are receiver-verified, but this version has no producer-authentication proof.

format.description: sft emits {completion, provenance} records per accepted item; preference emits chosen/rejected pairs from expert overrides and disagreeing ensemble minority votes; rlvr emits the executable reward — the task family's deterministic wasm verifier spec (program text, digest, input projection, pass/fail labels) — with the authoritative label demoted to reference metadata.

trainFraction.description: Deterministic content-addressed split threshold (default 0.9): a hash bucket of the artifact's raw bytes below the fraction lands in train, otherwise val. Identical content always splits identically.

includeRaw.description: Inline each artifact's {artifactType, rawBody} into records, per record capped at 256 KiB canonical JSON (oversized inputs are replaced by {rawOmitted, rawSha256}); the page also observes an overall byte budget.

pageToken.description: Keyset token — the last processed artifactName from the previous page.

pairSources.description: format=preference only — select the authority mix instead of a silent blend: expert_override is human feedback (RLHF-grade), ensemble_minority is AI feedback (RLAIF-grade). Defaults to both.

snapshotHash.description: The manifest snapshot the page was derived from.

items.description: Every record carries artifactName, artifactRawSha256, and split (train|val). sft records add completion {label, rationale} and provenance {verifier, determinism, humanResolved, labelName}. preference records add pairSource (expert_override | ensemble_minority) and chosen/rejected {label, rationale, verifier}. rlvr records add reward {kind: cradle_wasm, programId, programWat, programSha256, inputProjection, labelPass, labelFail} and reference {label, rationale, verifier, determinism, humanResolved}. includeRaw adds input {artifactType, rawBody} or {artifactType, rawOmitted, rawSha256}.

artifactIds.description: Full artifact resource names to profile; defaults to every artifact in the project.

duplicateGroups.description: Artifacts sharing an identical raw_hash, sorted by hash.

confidence.description: Stats over the latest label per artifact (0.0 when nothing is labeled).

labelValueCounts.description: Class balance over the latest label per artifact.

valid.description: True when the report contains no error-severity issues.

productIds.description: Exactly two product ids to compare (e.g. a train and an eval bundle).

overlapRatioA.description: Overlap divided by the count of distinct raw hashes in bundle A.

overlappingRawHashes.description: Sorted overlapping raw hashes, capped at 50.

determinismCounts.description: Label counts per provenance determinism value (deterministic, heuristic, model).

disagreements.description: Artifacts with more than one label whose values differ, capped at 50.

requiredEnv.description: Env var NAMES the connector reads (never their values).

missingEnv.description: Required env var names currently absent (empty when configured).

config.description: Non-secret config (region, endpointHost, accountHost, instanceHost, apiVersion).

prefix.description: s3 only; list objects under this key prefix.

key.description: s3 only; extract exactly this object key instead of listing.

statement.description: snowflake only, required for snowflake. Must start with SELECT, SHOW, or DESCRIBE; DML/DDL is rejected.

soql.description: salesforce only, required for salesforce. Must start with SELECT.

artifactType.description: Artifact type for ingested records. Defaults per connector (S3_OBJECT, SNOWFLAKE_ROW_BATCH, SALESFORCE_ROW_BATCH).

source.description: Artifact source label; defaults to the connector name.

ingest.description: When false, returns extracted payloads without storing artifacts.

rawBody.description: Present only when ingest=false (dry extraction preview).

inputSchema.description: JSON Schema for the tool's arguments; defaults to an open object.

implementation.description: deterministic_wasm: {wat: <WebAssembly text (module ...)>} executed in cradle. llm_prompt: {template: <prompt with {{arg}} placeholders>} rendered with the invocation arguments only (no secret interpolation). regex: {pattern, operation: match|extract|replace|split, replacement?, flags?} compiled stdlib re with catastrophic-backtracking shapes rejected at admission and execution wall-clock-bounded in a child process. chain: {steps: [2-8 existing non-chain tool names]} executed in order, the previous step's object output becoming the next step's arguments. The whole definition is capped at 64 KiB canonical JSON.

programSha256.description: Content digest of the implementation: the WAT program (deterministic_wasm), the prompt template (llm_prompt), or the canonical implementation JSON (regex, chain).

version.description: Monotonic; updates create a new version row and previous versions are retained.

arguments.description: Tool arguments per its stored inputSchema, capped at 64 KiB canonical JSON. deterministic_wasm tools take one integer 'n' (the run(i64) -> i64 cradle entrypoint).

determinism.description: deterministic_wasm and regex -> deterministic; llm_prompt -> model (heuristic in offline dry-run); chain -> deterministic only when every step was deterministic.

inputsDigest.description: deterministic_wasm: the cradle sandbox content-addressed inputs digest. llm_prompt: sha256 over the canonical {tool, version, arguments} projection.

output.description: deterministic_wasm: {status, value, passed}; llm_prompt: {label, confidence, rationale}; regex: operation-shaped ({matched, match, groups} | {matches} | {text, replacements} | {parts}); chain: {steps: [per-step provenance and output], output: <last step>}.
